en.question: If 2^x=3^y=6^(-z), find 1/x+1/y+1/z.
en.explanation: Let 2^x=3^y=6^(-z)=k. x=log k/log 2, y=log k/log 3, -z=log k/log 6. So 1/x+1/y+1/z=(log 2/log k)+(log 3/log k)-(log 6/log k)=0.

en.question: If 1/(∛4+∛2+1)=a∛4+b∛2+c, find a+b+c.
en.explanation: Multiply numerator and denominator by (∛2-1): Denominator becomes (∛4+∛2+1)(∛2-1)=∛8-1=2-1=1. So expression=∛2-1. a=0,b=1,c=-1. Sum=0.
